headline: Europe Slaps $3.5 Billion Fine On Google, Donald Trump Says Money Could Instead Go To American Investments and Jobs
summary: The European Commission fined Google €2.95 billion for breaching antitrust rules by favouring its own adtech services and limiting competition. Google must propose fixes within 60 days.

headline: Streaming Shift Forces Paramount to Rethink Cable Networks' Future
summary: Paramount Skydance is working to revive MTV and other networks by expanding digital content, investing in premium shows, and cutting costs as viewers shift from cable to streaming services.

headline: Figma And Lululemon Are Among Top 10 Large Cap Losers Last Week (September 1- September 5): Are The Others In Your Portfolio?
summary: Figma, Lululemon, Duolingo, and other big names led last week's top 10 large-cap stock declines across sectors.

headline: As Waymo Begins Airport Testing Operations In San Jose, This User Compared The Fare Of Regular Uber Rides Vs Tesla Robotaxi
summary: Waymo expands testing at San Jose airport with plans for commercial launch this year as Tesla rolls out Robotaxi app.

headline: Consumer Tech News (September 1 – September 5): Apple AI Push, Palantir Expands Partnerships, Tesla Robotaxi Surge, NVIDIA's $1.5B Chip Deal
summary: U.S. jobs stagnation, Fed rate-cut bets, record gold, major earnings, and tech–AI–auto shakeups dominated Wall Street this week.

headline: iPhone Price Hike, Siri AI Upgrades, Buffett Criticism And More: This Week In Appleverse
summary: Apple is making headlines with expected iPhone price hikes, a Siri upgrade, a new AI search tool, and a lawsuit over alleged misuse of copyrighted books.

headline: Elon Musk Snubs Trump Event, Google Hit With $425 Million Privacy Payout, Dan Ives Sees Apple-Gemini AI 'Dream Scenario' — This Week In Tech
summary: The weekend brought major tech and political headlines, from Elon Musk missing a White House event and Trump threatening tariffs on foreign chipmakers to Google facing a $425 million privacy penalty and Apple and Google scoring a big antitrust win.

headline: Trump's Tariffs, Wall Street's Fear Gauge And A Veteran Economist's Dire Warning: This Week In Economy
summary: Markets kicked off September on shaky ground as tariffs, weak jobs data, and warnings of a global collapse rattled investor confidence and fueled volatility.

headline: Trump Warns Of $15 Trillion Investment Cancellation, Mcconnell Compares Tariff Era To 1930s And More: This Week In Politics
summary: Trump warns of a $15T investment pullout, McConnell draws 1930s parallels, Musk gets snubbed at the White House, and Cuban cautions about political fallout.

headline: Microsoft Warns Azure Users Of Latency From Red Sea Cable Damage
summary: Microsoft warned that Azure users may face slower speeds due to undersea cable cuts in the Red Sea, but services remain online through rerouted traffic.

headline: Trump Explores Federal Takeover of 9/11 Memorial And Museum, NY Governor Kathy Hochul Calls For Resoring 'Health Care Funding' Instead
summary: White House considers federal takeover of 9/11 Memorial and Museum. Gov. Hochul opposes and Museum Leadership questions rationale.

headline: As Elon Musk's Pay Package Surges To $1 Trillion, Investor Urges Tesla To Adopt Political Neutrality: 'Elon Musk Is A Technical Genius'
summary: Tesla investor calls for political neutrality, citing risks of alienating customers and regulators. Board opposes proposal, citing free speech concerns.

headline: Elon Musk Announces Tesla AI5 Chip Design Review Success Amid Global Semiconductor Competition
summary: Tesla is advancing its custom AI chip development, focusing silicon talent on one powerful chip to strengthen its position.
